2020-11-01.title: 8 Pro-Democracy Politicians Arrested in Hong Kong Over Heated Meeting
2020-11-01.abstract: The Hong Kong politicians were arrested in connection with a contentious meeting in May, when the local legislature’s chambers were filled with protest over a battle for control over a key committee.

2020-11-01.title: Halloween Stabbing Attack in Quebec City Leaves 2 Dead
2020-11-01.abstract: The police arrested a 24-year-old man who they said was dressed in medieval garb and carrying a sword when he went on a rampage.

2020-11-01.title: In Italy, Like Everywhere the Virus Goes, It’s the Discontent That’s Contagious
2020-11-01.abstract: Italians are now a long way away from the days of singing on balconies and their summer fling with freedom. Just like everyone else, they’ve had it.

2020-11-01.title: Enzo Mari, industrial designer who kept things simple, dies at 88.
2020-11-01.abstract: This obituary is part of a series about people who have died in the coronavirus pandemic. Read about others here.

2020-11-01.title: For Australians, geographic isolation is now something to embrace.
2020-11-01.abstract: They used to call it “the tyranny of distance.”

2020-11-02.title: The Best Movies and TV Shows New to Netflix, Amazon and Stan in Australia in November
2020-11-02.abstract: Our streaming picks for November, including ‘The Crown,’ ‘Hillbilly Elegy’ and ‘Small Axe.’

2020-11-02.title: On Afghan Highways, Even the Police Fear the Taliban’s Toll Collectors
2020-11-02.abstract: As the U.S. retreats and peace talks stall, the Taliban have increasingly taken over Afghanistan’s highways, raising millions in unofficial taxes and strengthening their negotiating position.

2020-11-02.title: Almost Like Clockwork, Talk of a Military Coup Follows Thai Protests
2020-11-02.abstract: “I see a coup as not a bad thing,” said one prominent royalist who publicly called for a military intervention to quell antigovernment rallies in Bangkok and to protect the monarchy.

2020-11-02.title: Mount Everest Empties as Covid-19 Strikes Tourism in Nepal
2020-11-02.abstract: The Himalayan country’s economy has taken a blow from a lack of climbers and a plunge in remittances as infections spread.

2020-11-02.title: Prince William had Covid-19 in April but kept it a secret.
2020-11-02.abstract: Prince William contracted Covid-19 in England in April but kept his diagnosis a secret, the BBC and The Sun, England’s largest paper, have reported, citing information from sources within Kensington Palace. A person close to the palace confirmed the accuracy of the reports, but did not offer additional details.

2020-11-03.title: Hundreds Die at Sea Off Senegal’s Coast on a Perilous Route to Europe
2020-11-03.abstract: Those attempting to migrate from West Africa to Europe are once again taking a route so dangerous it’s known as “Barcelona or die.”

2020-11-03.title: ‘Clearly, Coloradans have lapsed’: New virus cases are setting records in Colorado.
2020-11-03.abstract: Colorado once looked like it had gotten a grip on the coronavirus. Gov. Jared Polis, a Democrat, is a zealous mask wearer who imposed a statewide mandate. Hospitalization levels stayed low for much of the summer and fall. The state even began dismantling two emergency field hospitals it set up early in the pandemic.

2020-11-03.title: Dozens Killed in Ethiopia in Schoolyard Massacre
2020-11-03.abstract: Rights groups said at least 54 people were killed in Sunday’s attack, which underscored how relations between Ethiopia’s ethnic groups are fraying.

2020-11-03.title: Hurricane Eta Strengthens as It Nears Central America
2020-11-03.abstract: The storm, set to make landfall late Monday or early Tuesday, could bring as much as 25 inches of rain to Nicaragua and Honduras. Flash floods and landslides were expected across the region.

2020-11-03.title: Vienna Terrorist Attack Leaves at Least 4 Dead and Many Wounded
2020-11-03.abstract: A gunman killed by the police was an Islamic State “sympathizer,” Austria’s interior minister said. Officers were searching for possible accomplices on Tuesday morning.

2020-11-04.title: Voters who saw containing the coronavirus as the most important issue favored Biden
2020-11-04.abstract: While President Trump had attempted to focus on anything other than the pandemic during the campaign, it remained the defining issue: More than 40 percent of voters said it was the most important issue deciding their vote, far more than any other issue.

2020-11-04.title: South Korea Detains North Korean Who Crossed Demilitarized Zone
2020-11-04.abstract: The man breached the Koreas’ heavily armed land border, triggering an extensive manhunt in the South, officials said.

2020-11-04.title: Having Made Peace Abroad, Ethiopia’s Leader Goes to War at Home
2020-11-04.abstract: Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, winner of the Nobel Peace Prize, began a sweeping military operation against one of his own regions.

2020-11-04.title: A Transfixed World Awaits What’s Next in America
2020-11-04.abstract: As U.S. results trickled in, they were analyzed far and wide with the sort of blanket news coverage most often reserved for elections closer to home.

2020-11-04.title: A New Front Opens in the Russia-Ukraine Conflict: Borscht
2020-11-04.abstract: “A lot of things were taken away from Ukraine, but they will not take our borscht,” said a chef who is leading a drive to recognize the soup as a Ukrainian cultural heritage.

2020-11-05.title: 100,000 cases in a single day push the U.S. into new terrain.
2020-11-05.abstract: The United States on Wednesday recorded over 100,000 new coronavirus cases in a single day for the first time since the pandemic began, bursting past a grim threshold even as the wave of infections engulfing the country shows no sign of receding.

2020-11-05.title: Norway’s Supreme Court Hears Rights Challenge to Arctic Oil Drilling
2020-11-05.abstract: Environmental groups argue that exploratory drilling licenses violate a constitutional right to a healthy environment. It’s a test case taking on an industry that is key to the country’s economy.

2020-11-05.title: Midwestern voters say they feared the virus, but some worried more about the economy.
2020-11-05.abstract: When Americans voted in this presidential election, they made it clear that of all the crucial issues facing the country, the coronavirus pandemic towered over the rest.

2020-11-05.title: The virus crisis dredges up a single mother’s old regrets and fears.
2020-11-05.abstract: Andrea Luttrell writes about the ways the pandemic has exacerbated her worries as a single mother, which came into sharper focus recently as she tried to juggle helping her 9-year-old son connect virtually to school and coping with the demands of her bosses and colleagues.

2020-11-05.title: Online posts from expats in France find an increased audience among stressed Americans.
2020-11-05.abstract: With American tourists banned from Europe, online posts from expatriates are as close to a vacation abroad as many got this year.

2020-11-06.title: Guatemala Mudslides Kill More Than 50
2020-11-06.abstract: Another 23 people across Central America have died or are missing as a result of a vast storm system dumping heavy rainfall across the region.

2020-11-06.title: American Tribalism, Australian Reflections
2020-11-06.abstract: Watching the United States choose a president from Sydney, our correspondent wonders: When will all the division end?

2020-11-06.title: A nasal spray prevented Covid-19 infection in ferrets, a study finds.
2020-11-06.abstract: A nasal spray that blocks the absorption of the coronavirus completely protected ferrets it was tested on, according to a small study released Thursday by an international team of scientists. The study, which was limited to animals and has not yet been peer-reviewed, was assessed by several health experts at the request of The New York Times.

2020-11-06.title: The Las Vegas Raiders are fined for repeatedly violating the N.F.L.’s virus protocols.
2020-11-06.abstract: The N.F.L. has fined the Las Vegas Raiders $500,000 and their head coach, Jon Gruden, an additional $150,000 and has taken away a late-round draft pick next year because of repeated violations of the league’s coronavirus protocols, according to a league employee who was not authorized to discuss the penalties publicly.

2020-11-06.title: A lockdown with loopholes: Some flout new restrictions in England.
2020-11-06.abstract: Chocolate shops and stationery stores were busy as usual. Universities held in-person lectures. And workers crowded into some offices and factories, often with nothing more than a bottle of communal hand sanitizer to protect them from the rampant spread of the coronavirus.

2020-11-07.title: ‘It’s Like Falling in Love’: Israeli Entrepreneurs Welcomed in Dubai
2020-11-07.abstract: A high-profile delegation of Israeli innovators visited the United Arab Emirates soon after moves to normalize relations.

2020-11-07.title: Along the Seine, Booksellers Try to Hold Off an Unhappy Ending
2020-11-07.abstract: The coronavirus lockdowns have taken a steep toll on Paris’s “bouquinistes,” whose bookstalls stretch for miles along the Seine. “We are barely making enough to eat.”

2020-11-07.title: String of Attacks Have People in Kabul Pointing a Finger, at the Government
2020-11-07.abstract: The assaults not only highlight a city under siege, they have exposed a growing, and very public, discontent with an Afghan government unable to protect its people.

2020-11-07.title: Biden to Face Long List of Foreign Challenges, With China No. 1
2020-11-07.abstract: President Trump will be handing Joseph R. Biden Jr. a difficult cleanup act in America’s relations with many countries. But it may not take much for Mr. Biden to improve the mood.

2020-11-07.title: Irish Home of Biden’s Great-Great-Great Grandfather Cheers His Victory
2020-11-07.abstract: He may be five generations removed, but the president-elect is a hometown boy in Ballina, Ireland.

2020-11-08.title: Chinese State Media Reacts to Biden Victory With Cautious Optimism
2020-11-08.abstract: Propaganda outlets said a Biden presidency would most likely restore stability to frayed U.S.-China relations, but warned that tensions would continue.

2020-11-08.title: A Famed Fig Tree’s Days Are Numbered as a New Highway Plows Through
2020-11-08.abstract: A tree that has graced a neighborhood in Nairobi, Kenya, for nearly a century is slated to be taken down to make way for a highway through the city intended to reduce traffic jams.

2020-11-08.title: Joy in India as Biden and Harris Win, but Questions, Too
2020-11-08.abstract: Kamala Harris’s ancestral village in southern India rejoiced on Sunday. But across the country, many wonder what changes the new administration will bring.

2020-11-08.title: A Norwegian soccer club wins as never before, while the pandemic distances its fans.
2020-11-08.abstract: This has been a golden year for the soccer club in Bodo, Norway, a city of 50,000 that sits at the southern end of the Arctic Circle. The club, Bodo/Glimt, stands on the cusp of claiming its first Norwegian championship.

2020-11-08.title: Europe Lauds Biden but Wonders: What Will He Want? How Long Will He Stay?
2020-11-08.abstract: Allies welcomed an American president who sees them as allies, not rivals. But with the U.S. so polarized, and with the possibility of a Republican-controlled Senate, they are also wary.

2020-11-09.title: Strong Turnout in Myanmar Shows Voters’ Support for Nascent Democracy
2020-11-09.abstract: Despite defections, Daw Aung San Suu Kyi’s party is expected to remain the strongest force in the Parliament. The military, which ruled for 50 years, still controls much of the government.

2020-11-09.title: France and Italy report record cases as virus ravages Europe.
2020-11-09.abstract: As winter approaches, Europe is struggling with a brutal resurgence of the virus, one that, along with the U.S. surge, has helped push the global case count over 50 million, according to a New York Times database. France and Italy have reported record daily case totals in recent days as both countries have moved to lock down some regions.

2020-11-09.title: Biden’s Win Means a Demotion for Netanyahu and Less Focus on Israel
2020-11-09.abstract: Benjamin Netanyahu has made his closeness to President Trump, who gave him much of what he wanted, central to his political appeal. Things will be different in a Biden administration.

2020-11-09.title: Buffeted by Trump, China Has Little Hope for Warmer Relations With Biden
2020-11-09.abstract: Many in Beijing believe that the United States will remain hostile to the country’s rise, regardless of who is president.

2020-11-09.title: ‘Stand by Her’: In China, a Movement Hands Out Free Sanitary Pads in Schools
2020-11-09.abstract: Students and teachers are placing pads outside bathrooms as part of a grass-roots campaign to fight period poverty and to remove the stigma surrounding menstruation.

2020-11-10.title: Peru President Is Impeached by Congress
2020-11-10.abstract: The vote to remove President Martín Vizcarra comes amid a devastating coronavirus pandemic. Mr. Vizcarra said he accepted the vote, reducing the likelihood of a constitutional crisis.

2020-11-10.title: Facing Military Debacle, Armenia Accepts a Deal in Nagorno-Karabakh War
2020-11-10.abstract: In an agreement brokered by Russia, Azerbaijan won many of the concessions it has sought for decades in negotiations over the Nagorno-Karabakh separatist region.

2020-11-10.title: India’s Modi Reverses His Fortunes in Crucial State
2020-11-10.abstract: Mr. Modi declares victory for his coalition in Bihar State, stopping the B.J.P.’s recent slide with a vote seen as a referendum on the government’s Covid-19 response.

2020-11-10.title: Hong Kong Protest Icon Mysteriously Vanished. Then She Returned, Unbowed.
2020-11-10.abstract: Alexandra Wong, 64, better known as “Grandma Wong,” said she had been detained by the Chinese authorities and made to pledge she would stop protesting.

2020-11-10.title: Who Would Rig This Vote? The Fraud Was Real (and Feathers Were Ruffled)
2020-11-10.abstract: More than 1,500 fake votes were slipped into New Zealand’s Bird of the Year 2020 contest in favor of the kiwi pukupuku.

2020-11-11.title: China Targets Hong Kong’s Lawmakers as It Squelches Dissent
2020-11-11.abstract: Four pro-democracy figures were forced out of the legislature, prompting another 15 to vow to resign in solidarity, as Beijing cracks down on one of the city’s few remaining venues for political opposition.

2020-11-11.title: Australia’s Struggle With ‘Bonk Bans’ and ‘Manterruptions’
2020-11-11.abstract: Prime Minister Scott Morrison came under fire this week for abruptly interrupting a female senior minister who was asked what it’s like to be a woman in Parliament.

2020-11-11.title: Judge Is Shot and Killed in Her Office at Manila’s City Hall
2020-11-11.abstract: The judge was identified as Maria Theresa Abadilla, and the mayor’s office said a court clerk had been named by the police as a suspect in the shooting.

2020-11-11.title: BBC Orders Inquiry Into Diana Interview After Claim Princess Was Misled
2020-11-11.abstract: A quarter-century after the princess admitted infidelity in a landmark interview with Martin Bashir, the journalist is facing renewed accusations of behaving unethically to secure the scoop.

2020-11-11.title: India’s Covid-19 Cases Have Plummeted. Many Fear a New Wave.
2020-11-11.abstract: While some hope the worst might be over, lapsed vigilance, cold weather and the festival season could spark a resurgence.

2020-11-12.title: How the Dream of Hong Kong Democracy Was Dimmed
2020-11-12.abstract: A look at key moments in the showdown between pro-democratic forces and the Beijing-backed authorities who have clamped down on Hong Kong’s freedoms.

2020-11-12.title: Newark’s mayor imposes new restrictions as the city reaches a positivity rate of 19%.
2020-11-12.abstract: Two weeks ago, as the coronavirus began to spread widely again in Newark, officials imposed New Jersey’s toughest new restrictions since the spring lockdown, mandating an 8 p.m. indoor closing time for all restaurants, bars and nonessential businesses citywide.

2020-11-12.title: Blackpink Cuddled a Baby Panda. Not Cute, the Chinese Internet Said.
2020-11-12.abstract: The K-pop band was accused of putting the cub at risk after the group posted video of its antics at a South Korean zoo.

2020-11-12.title: A Police Swarm. Frantic Calls. Then 3,000 People Locked Inside.
2020-11-12.abstract: On July 4, Australia’s second-largest city went into lockdown. But residents of nine public housing towers were singled out for even stricter treatment, leading many to suspect discrimination.

2020-11-12.title: Gunman Fires on Saudi Embassy in The Hague
2020-11-12.abstract: No one was injured in the rare attack on a diplomatic mission in the Netherlands.

2020-11-13.title: Argentina to Allow Medicinal Marijuana to Be Grown at Home
2020-11-13.abstract: The new regulation also orders insurance providers to cover cannabis products prescribed by doctors. Some of the strongest champions of the government’s move were mothers of sick children.

2020-11-13.title: Reconsidering Melbourne’s Public Housing Lockdown
2020-11-13.abstract: Why the harshest example of an already-harsh lockdown had left many with a lingering sense of discrimination.

2020-11-13.title: A Solution to Pandemic Hunger, Eyeballs and All
2020-11-13.abstract: A Maori community center in New Zealand is distributing bags of donated fish heads to families in need. But it’s more than just charity; it’s a model for reducing food waste.

2020-11-13.title: China Congratulates Biden on Presidential Victory
2020-11-13.abstract: “We respect the choice of the American people,” a spokesman for the country’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs said, ending days of conspicuous silence about the election result.

2020-11-13.title: From ‘Street Kid’ in India to Top Toronto Chef
2020-11-13.abstract: After a rugged childhood, Sash Simpson realized his dream last year when he opened a high-end restaurant. Within months, the pandemic struck.

2020-11-14.title: Al Qaeda’s No. 2, Accused in U.S. Embassy Attacks, Was Killed in Iran
2020-11-14.abstract: Israeli agents shot Abu Muhammad al-Masri on the streets of Tehran at the behest of the U.S., officials said, but no one — Iran, Al Qaeda, the U.S. or Israel — has publicly acknowledged the killing.

2020-11-14.title: Iota, Now a Hurricane, Is Season’s 30th Named Storm
2020-11-14.abstract: A Category One storm, Iota was expected to strengthen into a major hurricane by Monday as it approached the coast of Central America, according to the National Hurricane Center.

2020-11-14.title: How a Human Rights Angel Lost Her Halo
2020-11-14.abstract: Ten years after she left house arrest and vowed to fight for justice, Myanmar’s civilian leader has instead become a jailer of critics and an apologist for the slaughter of minorities.

2020-11-14.title: Rome Tracks Down the Man Behind All That Graffiti. No, It’s Not Banksy.
2020-11-14.abstract: The tagger known as Geco is not as famous as the British provocateur, but he has made a name for himself in Italy.

2020-11-14.title: ‘You Cannot Say No’: The Reign of Terror That Sustains Belarus’s Leader
2020-11-14.abstract: Despite hundreds of thousands protesting against him for months, Aleksandr G. Lukashenko has been able to cling to power thanks to an all-pervasive security system.

2020-11-15.title: Her Abuse Was a ‘Family Matter,’ Until It Went Live
2020-11-15.abstract: The grisly death of a farmer, whose attack was streamed online, has shocked China, with many asking why the legal system failed to protect her.

2020-11-15.title: They Once Ruled Ethiopia. Now They Are Fighting Its Government.
2020-11-15.abstract: Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed’s two-year feud with the rebellious ruling party of the Tigray region has exploded into a war, with bombings, massacres and ethnic divisions, that threatens to upend the entire Horn of Africa.

2020-11-15.title: With Progressive Politics on March in New Zealand, Maori Minister Blazes New Trails
2020-11-15.abstract: Nanaia Mahuta, the new foreign minister, brings a reputation as an honest broker to Prime Minister Jacinda Ardern’s cabinet, the most diverse in the country’s history.

2020-11-15.title: After War Between Armenia and Azerbaijan, Peace Sees Winners and Losers Swap Places
2020-11-15.abstract: A deal brokered by Russia ended the fighting for now over Nagorno-Karabakh, leaving Armenians to pack up and burn their houses as they retreat, while Azerbaijanis plan a return to long-lost lands.

2020-11-15.title: Egypt Unearths New Mummies Dating Back 2,500 Years
2020-11-15.abstract: More than 100 painted wooden coffins, many with bodies, were found in the necropolis of Saqqara, officials said. After several recent finds at the site, it’s the largest discovery there this year.

2020-11-16.title: Japanese City Uses ‘Monster Wolf’ to Scare Off Wild Bears
2020-11-16.abstract: Officials in Japan installed a “Monster Wolf” near a residential neighborhood after wild bears became a nuisance, increasing the risk of deadly encounters with humans.

2020-11-16.title: Japanese ‘Monster Wolf’ Aims to Scare Off Wild Bears
2020-11-16.abstract: After bears kept venturing into the city in search of food, officials installed mechanical wolves with bared teeth, glowing red eyes and a fearsome screech to keep the animals away.

2020-11-16.title: Spain’s Other Covid Casualties: Undetected Cancer Cases
2020-11-16.abstract: A raft of lawsuits has emerged from a health care system where the struggle to fight the pandemic has led to neglect of other serious conditions.

2020-11-16.title: Britain’s prime minister is quarantining, and other news around the world.
2020-11-16.abstract: Seven months after he battled a serious case of Covid-19, the disease caused by the coronavirus, Prime Minister Boris Johnson of Britain announced on Sunday that he was quarantining after coming into contact with a lawmaker later found to be infected.

2020-11-16.title: Division Among the Democrats
2020-11-16.abstract: After the party lost seats in the House of Representatives on Election Day, some are pointing fingers and debating which wing was at fault.

2020-11-17.title: Store Accused of Fat Shaming for Calling Large-Size Women’s Clothing ‘Rotten’
2020-11-17.abstract: The Taiwanese chain RT-Mart apologized for a measurement chart in a store in the Chinese mainland that described bigger sizes as “Rotten,” “Extra Rotten” and “Rotten to the Core.”

2020-11-17.title: California is ‘pulling the emergency brake’ on its reopening plans.
2020-11-17.abstract: California’s governor announced Monday that the state is “pulling the emergency brake” on its reopening and reinstating broad restrictions, while Iowa’s governor reversed course and announced a mask mandate.

2020-11-17.title: Hurricane Iota Poses Threat of Floods and Landslides in Central America
2020-11-17.abstract: The storm has left three dead, and is moving across parts of Central America that are still reeling from Hurricane Eta.

2020-11-17.title: In China, people scramble for shots of unproven vaccines.
2020-11-17.abstract: China has made its unproven vaccine candidates widely available to demonstrate their safety and effectiveness to a country that has long been skeptical of vaccines after a spate of quality scandals.

2020-11-17.title: German Police Arrest 3 Suspects in Heist of Royal Jewels
2020-11-17.abstract: Officers searched homes, garages and vehicles in Berlin, detaining two 23-year-olds and a 26-year-old in connection with the theft of gold, diamonds and other precious stones in Dresden last year.

2020-11-18.title: U.S. Troops Are Packing Up, Ready or Not
2020-11-18.abstract: The Pentagon has announced a reduction down to 2,500 U.S. troops in Afghanistan before President-elect Biden takes office. Afghan officials fear the cuts will encourage the Taliban to keep fighting.

2020-11-18.title: ‘Within Seconds Everything Was Gone’: Devastating Floods Submerge the Philippines
2020-11-18.abstract: Torrential rains and back-to-back typhoons have ripped through the country in the past two weeks, turning a once picturesque river into a sea of murky brown, killing dozens and setting off deadly landslides.

2020-11-18.title: The vaccines will probably work. Making them fast will be the hard part.
2020-11-18.abstract: The promising news that not just one but two coronavirus vaccines were more than 90 percent effective in early results has buoyed hopes that an end to the pandemic is in sight.

2020-11-18.title: F.D.A. authorizes the first at-home coronavirus test.
2020-11-18.abstract: The Food and Drug Administration on Tuesday gave an emergency green light to the first rapid coronavirus test that can run from start to finish at home, paving a potential path for more widespread testing outside of health care settings.

2020-11-18.title: Abandoning its loose approach to virus controls, Sweden clamps down.
2020-11-18.abstract: In the spring, as European governments imposed draconian lockdown orders that turned capitals into ghost towns and forced people to largely confine themselves to their homes, Sweden opted for a lighter touch.

2020-11-19.title: Landslide in Ecuador Kills 5 at Illegal Mine
2020-11-19.abstract: At least two more people were missing. The deaths, including that of a minor, occurred as the region was struggling to combat the illegal gold industry.

2020-11-19.title: Blood Lust and Demigods: Behind an Australian Force’s Slaughter of Helpless Afghans
2020-11-19.abstract: The findings of a four-year military inquiry paint a brutal picture of a special forces culture of rewarding the killing of innocents and prisoners and methodically covering it up.

2020-11-19.title: The Coronavirus Is Airborne Indoors. Why Are We Still Scrubbing Surfaces?
2020-11-19.abstract: Scientists who initially warned about contaminated surfaces now say that the virus spreads primarily through inhaled droplets, and that there is little to no evidence that deep cleaning mitigates the threat indoors.

2020-11-19.title: Seeking Restart With Biden, Palestinians Eye End to Prisoner Payments
2020-11-19.abstract: The Palestinian Authority pays stipends to prisoners held by Israel in a system critics call “pay to slay.” The worse the crime, the higher the payment.

2020-11-19.title: A Chinese County Aims to Curb Dog Walking by Threatening to Kill the Dog
2020-11-19.abstract: Restrictions on dogs are common in China. But one county’s plans to uniformly ban dog walking and kill the pets of rule-breaking owners has shocked the internet.

2020-11-20.title: Margaret Guilfoyle, a Multiple ‘First’ in Australia Politics, Dies at 94
2020-11-20.abstract: As a cabinet member from 1975 to 1983, she was the first woman to run an Australian government department. Her country’s news media called her “a mother with political ambitions.”

2020-11-20.title: Amid the Coronavirus Pandemic, a Battle Emerges Over Green Spaces
2020-11-20.abstract: A need for parks during lockdown has turned into a debate about the future of inner-city golf courses.

2020-11-20.title: Under a Divisive Peace, Wartime Rifts Hobble Hope in Bosnia
2020-11-20.abstract: The Dayton Accords, which ended fighting in the country 25 years ago, created a dysfunctional system that put power in the hands of politicians stoking ethnic division.

2020-11-20.title: 1945: Britain Asks for Whiskey to Ensure ‘Merry, Merry Christmas’
2020-11-20.abstract: For the first peacetime Christmas after World War II, a British newspaper called for whiskey usually exported to America to be kept aside to boost people’s spirits at home.

2020-11-20.title: A senior Defense Department official is infected with the virus, and others were exposed.
2020-11-20.abstract: Anthony J. Tata, a senior Pentagon official, has tested positive for the coronavirus after meeting with a visiting Lithuanian official who also met with the acting American defense secretary and other high-level U.S. military officials.

2020-11-21.title: U.S. records 12 millionth case as virus surge gathers speed.
2020-11-21.abstract: The United States passed 11 million total coronavirus cases on Sunday, and its caseload has now soared past 12 million. New daily cases are approaching 200,000: on Friday, the country recorded more than 198,500, a record.

2020-11-21.title: Two Chinese port cities mobilize after finding a handful of cases.
2020-11-21.abstract: Two of China’s largest port cities, Shanghai and Tianjin, have announced locally transmitted coronavirus infections, renewing worries about whether the country can continue to keep out the virus after coming close to eradicating it over the summer.

2020-11-21.title: As Pompeo Prepared to Meet Afghan Warring Parties, New Attack Struck Kabul
2020-11-21.abstract: Secretary of State Mike Pompeo, in Doha, Qatar, met with representatives of the Afghan government and the Taliban, who are at an impasse in peace talks.

2020-11-21.title: Saudi Arabia and Human Rights Activists Fight Over Kingdom’s Image at G-20
2020-11-21.abstract: As the summit’s host, the kingdom hopes to showcase its advances, while critics see an opportunity to pounce. But the virtual event will have a lower profile.

2020-11-21.title: How a Man With a Van Is Challenging U.K. Drug Policy
2020-11-21.abstract: A former drug user turned activist is addressing Scotland’s alarming drug death crisis by running the nation’s first drug consumption room — and risking arrest to do it.

2020-11-22.title: The latest virus wave appears to have crested in Europe, but the struggle is far from over.
2020-11-22.abstract: The latest wave of the coronavirus has inundated Europe after a summer in which many of its countries appeared to have brought the pandemic under control more effectively than the Americas did.

2020-11-22.title: Protesters in Guatemala Set Fire to Congress Building Over Spending Cuts
2020-11-22.abstract: After two hurricanes displaced thousands of people and wrecked infrastructure, a bill that cut spending on health and education and bolstered lawmakers’ meal stipends touched off a wave of anger in the capital.

2020-11-22.title: South Dakota and New Mexico offer a snapshot of the alternate realities in the U.S. pandemic.
2020-11-22.abstract: Known coronavirus cases in the United States have now surpassed 12 million, and the daily routines of a vast number of Americans are now shaped by their ZIP codes and governors and beliefs about the virus. Do they wear masks? Go to school in person or online? Eat out? Get exposed to the virus?

2020-11-22.title: Along Russia’s ‘Road of Bones,’ Relics of Suffering and Despair
2020-11-22.abstract: The Kolyma Highway in the Russian Far East once delivered tens of thousands of prisoners to the work camps of Stalin’s gulag. The ruins of that cruel era are still visible today.

2020-11-22.title: N.Y.C. says 3 percent of its coronavirus tests reveal infections. Why does the state disagree?
2020-11-22.abstract: A 3 percent positive rate in coronavirus testing is a critical threshold for New York City. It is the point at which the mayor shut down public schools last week. The governor says that a sustained 3 percent level in the city will result in banning indoor dining, closing gyms and hair salons, and placing a 25-person cap on attendance at houses of worship even as the holidays approach.

2020-11-23.title: Mass testing at a Shanghai airport causes chaos.
2020-11-23.abstract: Since the pandemic erupted in China, the country has grown adept at swiftly smothering coronavirus flare-ups by ordering residents across entire cities to line up for nucleic acid tests that can pinpoint carriers. So officials snapped into action after a cluster of infections linked to Pudong International Airport in Shanghai grew over the weekend.

2020-11-23.title: Daniel Cordier, French Resistance Hero, Is Dead at 100
2020-11-23.abstract: He parachuted into occupied France and became an assistant to the legendary Jean Moulin, though only later did he learn Moulin’s true identity.

2020-11-23.title: Joshua Wong Pleads Guilty Over 2019 Hong Kong Protest
2020-11-23.abstract: He and two fellow activists, Agnes Chow and Ivan Lam, were immediately jailed on charges of unauthorized assembly. They will be sentenced next week.

2020-11-23.title: It Was Just Him and His Smiley Face. He’s Charged With Illegal Assembly.
2020-11-23.abstract: Jolovan Wham, a civil rights activist in Singapore, was charged with violating the Public Order Act for holding up a cardboard sign with a smiley face on it near a police station in March.

2020-11-23.title: France’s Dragnet for Extremists Sweeps Up Some Schoolchildren, Too
2020-11-23.abstract: When France held a tribute to a teacher killed after a class on freedom of expression, the police interrogated at least 14 students over comments flagged as inappropriate.

2020-11-24.title: In Canada, a Push to Keep Schools Open in Second Lockdown
2020-11-24.abstract: Like many places in Europe — and in contrast to New York and other big American cities — school districts in Canada’s largest province and many elsewhere are finding in-class learning outweighs the risks.

2020-11-24.title: Why Were Canadians Warned Not to Let Moose Lick Their Cars?
After Trump, Biden Faces Pressure to Stand Up to China by Embracing Taiwan
2020-11-24.abstract: In his final days in office, President Trump is trying to undermine Beijing by showing support for Taiwan. President-elect Biden will likely follow a similar path — without the pugnacity.

2020-11-24.title: Qatar Charges Airport Police After Female Passengers Are Strip-Searched
2020-11-24.abstract: Officials also said they had identified the mother of a baby abandoned at the airport, and that they were seeking to extradite her and charge her with attempted murder.

2020-11-24.title: Evidence Builds That an Early Mutation Made the Pandemic Harder to Stop
2020-11-24.abstract: Scientists were initially skeptical that a mutation made the coronavirus more contagious. But new research has changed many of their minds.

2020-11-25.title: U.N. Gets OK to Aid Crippled Yemen Tanker After Months of Waiting
2020-11-25.abstract: Officials have been waiting since July to inspect and repair the FSO Safer, a stricken tanker off the Yemen coast. Houthi rebels have finally given approval, the U.N. said.

2020-11-25.title: Death by Shark Is at a High in Australia. What’s Going On?
2020-11-25.abstract: Eight people have been fatally attacked this year, the most in nearly a century. But scientists say these numbers don’t tell the full story.

2020-11-25.title: A Chinese firm seeks permission to market a vaccine before it has completed trials.
2020-11-25.abstract: A Chinese state-owned vaccine maker has filed an application with the country’s Food and Drug Administration to market coronavirus vaccines in China before the completion of late-stage trials that will determine their safety and efficacy.

2020-11-25.title: Casualties From Banned Cluster Bombs Nearly Doubled in 2019, Mostly in Syria
2020-11-25.abstract: The war in Syria, which began almost a decade ago, makes it the only country where the outlawed bombs have been repeatedly used since 2012.

2020-11-25.title: May Madness? Postponing the tournament is possible as college basketball begins.
2020-11-25.abstract: Amid numerous game cancellations and players and coaches alike testing positive for the coronavirus, the men’s college basketball season will start Wednesday with more than 100 games being played nationwide. This burst of games and the travel — some of which will happen on commercial flights — come at a time when universities are urging their non-basketball-playing students to exercise great caution as they head home for Thanksgiving.

2020-11-26.title: South Korean Mastermind of Exploitation Chat Rooms Gets 40 Years in Prison
2020-11-26.abstract: Cho Joo-bin was convicted of blackmailing young women into making sexually explicit video clips that he sold online.

2020-11-26.title: In Argentina, Maradona’s Death Sets Off a Vivid Display of Public Grief
2020-11-26.abstract: For many Argentines, Diego Maradona, who died on Wednesday, was no mere soccer superstar. “I feel like a member of my family just died,” said one.

2020-11-26.title: Turkey Jails Hundreds for Life Over 2016 Coup Attempt
2020-11-26.abstract: Those accused of being ringleaders were among the many sentenced in one of the most important mass trials relating to the plot.

2020-11-26.title: ‘The Crown’ Stokes an Uproar Over Fact vs. Entertainment
2020-11-26.abstract: Dramatic liberties in the latest season of the Netflix series, covering the turbulent 1980s, are annoying Britons who wrote of that period, even among those who disparage the royals.

2020-11-26.title: Times readers shared what they’re thankful for in six words.
2020-11-26.abstract: Last week, David Leonhardt invited readers of his Morning newsletter to send six words describing what made them thankful in 2020. Here is a selection of their responses:

2020-11-27.title: Argentina, and Latin America, Mourn Maradona as a Man of the People
2020-11-27.abstract: Known for siding with leftist leaders and causes, the soccer legend’s life, and his politics, never strayed far from his impoverished roots, and his fans loved him for it.

2020-11-27.title: Six U.S. Oil Executives Are Convicted of Corruption in Venezuela
2020-11-27.abstract: The so-called Citgo 6, who had been lured to Venezuela three years ago for a business meeting, were immediately sentenced to prison.

2020-11-27.title: Escaping the Virus, Almost
2020-11-27.abstract: How a planned two-week trip to Sydney from Melbourne stretched into a five-month drive across Australia dictated by lockdowns and coronavirus restrictions.

2020-11-27.title: Australian Military Moves to Dismiss Soldiers After Killings in Afghanistan
2020-11-27.abstract: A scathing report revealed that members of the special forces had waged a campaign to cover up unlawful killings of Afghan civilians.

2020-11-27.title: A Mother, Her Son — and Their 1,500-Mile Search for Home
2020-11-27.abstract: Millions of people have been displaced by the pandemic. Jessika and her 6-year-old, Sebastián, are among the many who have yet to find a home.

2020-11-28.title: Gunmen Assassinate Iran’s Top Nuclear Scientist in Ambush, Provoking New Crisis
2020-11-28.abstract: Iran expressed fury over the killing of Mohsen Fakhrizadeh, blaming it on Israel and the United States. His death may complicate President-elect Biden’s intention to restore the Iranian nuclear deal.

2020-11-28.title: Macron Says Images of French Police Beating Black Man ‘Shame Us’
2020-11-28.abstract: President Emmanuel Macron called for reaffirming “the link of confidence that should naturally exist between the French and those who protect them.”

2020-11-28.title: Beijing Takes Its South China Sea Strategy to the Himalayas
2020-11-28.abstract: China built a village in territory also claimed by the kingdom of Bhutan, echoing its aggressive tactics at the border with India and in places much farther away.

2020-11-28.title: Pedophile Scandal Can’t Crack the Closed Circles of Literary France
2020-11-28.abstract: The scandal surrounding the writer Gabriel Matzneff was not limited to his pedophilia. It also opened a window on the entrenched and clubby nature of many of France’s elite institutions.

2020-11-28.title: As Iran Threatens Payback After Assassination, Germany Urges Restraint
2020-11-28.abstract: The killing of Iran’s top nuclear scientist, which Tehran blamed on Israel, raised fears of an escalation in violent retribution.

2020-11-29.title: The Lost Days That Made Bergamo a Coronavirus Tragedy
2020-11-29.abstract: The northern Italian province became one of the deadliest killing fields for the virus in the Western world. But a Times investigation found that faulty guidance and bureaucratic delays rendered the toll far worse than it had to be.

2020-11-29.title: With Art Basel canceled, Miami looks inward — and the show goes on.
2020-11-29.abstract: Art Basel Miami Beach, the annual fair that usually draws the contemporary art world to South Florida and was scheduled for this week, has been canceled — a cultural casualty of the coronavirus.

2020-11-29.title: Leadership of U.N. Human Rights Body Becomes Proxy Battle for World Powers
2020-11-29.abstract: A fight over the council’s presidency is seen by some as an effort by China, Russia and Saudi Arabia to put a more compliant state in control ahead of greater involvement by the Biden administration.

2020-11-29.title: Plan to Hold Corporations Liable for Violations Abroad Fails in Switzerland
2020-11-29.abstract: The proposal would have exposed multinational companies to greater scrutiny over human rights and environmental abuses.

2020-11-29.title: Offices of Diego Maradona’s Doctor Raided Over His Death
2020-11-29.abstract: The authorities searched the home and offices of the soccer hero’s doctor as part of an investigation into the circumstances of his death last week.

2020-11-30.title: New Zealand Announces Charges in Deadly White Island Volcano Eruption
2020-11-30.abstract: Government agencies and individuals were among those charged over the roles in the 2019 disaster, which killed 22 people.

2020-11-30.title: Hong Kong’s Courts Are Still Independent. Some Want to Rein Them In.
2020-11-30.abstract: The judiciary is crucial to the city’s status as a global hub for trade and finance. But the Chinese Communist Party has been gaining more authority over it.

2020-11-30.title: Australia Condemns Lurid Tweet by Chinese Official as ‘Disgusting Slur’
2020-11-30.abstract: The post, with a doctored photograph showing an Australian soldier with a knife to the throat of an Afghan child, sent relations between the two countries to a new low.

2020-11-30.title: 4 Officers in France Are Charged Over Beating of a Black Man
2020-11-30.abstract: The violence against Michel Zecler, a 41-year old music producer, caused a national outcry and fueled longstanding accusations of police brutality and racism.

2020-11-30.title: As Iran Mourns Nuclear Scientist, Officials Vow to Find His Killers
2020-11-30.abstract: “We chase the criminals to the end,” the Iranian defense minister said at the funeral of Mohsen Fakhrizadeh.
